description: Machine-readable autonomy gradient for the (future) LLM drift classifier. The classifier earns exactly ONE hop — classifying an already-detected diff's materiality + contract owner — and every action it could drive is gated here. Enforced by scripts/classifier-walls.py (the only reader); current earned tier lives in earned-tier.json (default advisory_comment, promoted ONLY by humans). This file is hash-pinned via .harness-hash-extra-patterns so a silent edit (e.g. flipping a `never` action) fails CI. Normative spec: 000-docs/055-AT-SPEC-llm-classifier-deterministic-walls-2026-06-12.md.

advisory_comment.description: Post an advisory comment carrying the schema-validated classification row on the existing drift/reconciliation issue. Always allowed — lowest blast radius: a wrong comment costs a human read, nothing else.

low_blast_pr.description: Open a low-blast-radius mechanical-refresh PR — snapshot/sha/coverage refreshes ONLY, never schema or policy content. Requires the earned tier in earned-tier.json, which itself requires the eval-set recall floor cleared.

earn_requires.note: drift-eval-runner.py ships separately (manifest-integrity + recall-floor scorer; exits nonzero below recall floor 1.0 on material cases). Every invocation by the walls script is guarded by a file-existence check, so this policy is enforceable both before and after that runner lands.

kernel_schema_edit.description: Editing kernel schemas (@intentsolutions/core schemas/authoring/v1) is NEVER autonomous, at any tier. The classifier may at most open an issue carrying a proposed diff; a HUMAN lands the change through intent-eval-core's own review + release gates (tier-3 promotion is human per 052-AT-SPEC).

close_drift_signal.description: Closing a drift signal (reconciliation issue, drift alert) is NEVER autonomous, at any tier. Only a human declares a drift reconciled — otherwise the classifier could silence the very signal that audits it.

promotion.protocol: A human runs the eval set (drift-eval-runner --verify-manifest, then --score over the candidate classifier's predictions), confirms the recall floor on material cases is cleared, then commits the updated earned-tier.json (tier + promoted_by + promoted_at + evidence) in a reviewed PR, re-running `scripts/audit-harness init` in the same commit because the tier state is hash-pinned. No script may write earned-tier.json; demotion is the same protocol in reverse and needs no evidence.
